assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all four types: 'Scheduled Upgrade Notifications', 'Upgrade Available Event' (new version), 'Minor Version End of Support' (end-of-life), and 'Security Patch Notifications'

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response assigns 'HIGH PRIORITY' to end-of-life warnings, not security patches. Security patches are marked as 'REVIEW - Usually Auto' and new version available is 'INFORMATIONAL - No Action Required', which doesn't match the expected priority ordering

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response clearly states for EoS warnings: 'Immediate: Plan upgrade within 30 days' and provides detailed action steps including checking EoS timeline and scheduling maintenance

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response includes specific pre-upgrade actions for scheduled upgrades: 'Run pre-upgrade health checks' and mentions 'Run pre-upgrade checklist' in the team playbook

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response states security patches 'auto-apply' and require 'No blocking action needed', which contradicts the expectation that they may require expedited manual upgrades

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response categorizes 'Upgrade Available Event' as 'Informational - No Action Required' and states 'Monitor only. Review release notes if interested in new features'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response includes specific section 'Configure Pub/Sub for Programmatic Processing' with commands to create topics and configure notifications, plus Cloud Logging queries

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Review release notes if interested in new features', 'Review breaking changes in release notes between current → next minor', and 'Review release notes for interesting features'
